finding_results.notes: The general-library survey now points Hermione to an archive-only catalogue mark, the retrieval happens through the open gated archive, and the folio is reshelved back into archive 14-C before Draco returns.
The chapter now ends on Hermione's conscious withholding in the library, with the lie named internally as concealment rather than routed into another insomnia close.
The scene opens on the new diagnostic pattern and immediate probe instead of re-teaching instrument layout, sleeve-rolling, or table setup.
The repeated 'sharp angles' facial descriptor was removed and replaced with a moment-specific observation about Draco's mouth under strain.

The only retained Mark-pain-adjacent beat is the new information that Hermione's touch has become bodily relief from Draco's side of the channel; the chapter no longer keeps re-establishing the baseline ache.

Literal resonance references were cut to the new bidirectional breach, the archive cross-reference, and the folio's technical explanation; the rest of the chapter now focuses on consequence rather than repeatedly naming the phenomenon.
The prose was recast with periods, commas, and colons. No em-dashes remain in the revised chapter.
Sentence entries were rebuilt around varied subjects and paragraph pivots. The revised chapter reduces She-open sentence starts to 9.

summary: All packet findings were addressed in the chapter revision: the exam opening now starts on new data, the bonding folio is canonically retrieved from the gated archive, repeated Mark/resonance language was compressed, em-dash and She-open density were reduced below target, and the chapter now closes on Hermione's active concealment rather than a bedroom insomnia tag.
